title: Inhaler and methods of use thereof
abstract: A medicament delivery device comprises a dosing chamber having an interior that is configured to contain dry powder medicament, and a transducer confronting the dosing chamber. Preferably, the dosing chamber and the transducer are acoustically resonant such that the dosing chamber is configured to resonate in response to an activation of the transducer. The dosing chamber may have an interior shape, internal height and location of one or more openings configured to enable the dry powder medicament to become aerosolized and delivered from the dosing chamber via synthetic jetting upon an activation of the transducer.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  I/We claim: 1. A medicament delivery device comprising: a dosing chamber comprising an interior that is configured to contain dry powder medicament, and a transducer confronting the dosing chamber, wherein the dosing chamber and the transducer are acoustically resonant such that the dosing chamber is configured to resonate in response to an activation of the transducer; wherein the dosing chamber has an interior shape, internal height and location of one or more openings configured to enable the dry powder medicament to become aerosolized and delivered from the dosing chamber via synthetic jetting upon an activation of the transducer, wherein the dosing chamber's interior shape is at least partially defined by a lower sidewall that transitions to a shoulder, the shoulder transitions to an apex extending away from the lower sidewall, and the apex converges to a point, wherein the one or more openings in the dosing chamber are disposed in the apex, and wherein the internal height of the dosing chamber is configured so that pressure oscillation at the one or more openings is sufficiently high to cause the dry powder medicament to become aerosolized and delivered from the one or more openings, wherein the dosing chamber' s interior shape and height are configured so that the combined acoustic resonance of the transducer and dosing chamber is sufficient to cause aerosolization and delivery of the dry powder medicament having an MMAD of about 6 Î¼ÏÎ¹ or less upon an activation of the transducer. 2. The medicament delivery device of claim 1 comprising a plurality of openings disposed in the apex, each of the plurality of openings having a centerpoint spaced substantially equidistantly on a circle, the circle having its centerpoint on an axis defined by a point formed by the apex. 3. The medicament delivery device of any of claims 1 to 2, wherein the apex has an apex wall thickness that is less than the thickness of the vertical sidewall. 4. The medicament delivery device of any of claims 1 to 2, wherein the apex has an apex wall thickness that is less than the wall thickness of the remainder of the dosing chamber. 5. The medicament delivery device of any of claims 1 to 4, wherein the internal height of the dosing chamber is between about 4 mm and about 6 mm, or between about 9 mm and about 1 1 mm, wherein the transducer has a resonance frequency between about 37 kHz and about 42 kHz. 6. The medicament delivery device of any of claims 1 to 5, wherein the one or more openings have a diameter of from about 0.01 inches (0.25 mm) to about 0.05 inches (1.3 mm). 7. The medicament delivery device of any of claims 1 to 6, wherein the dosing chamber's interior shape and height are configured so that the combined acoustic resonance of the transducer and dosing chamber is sufficient to cause aerosolization and delivery of the dry powder medicament having a fine particle fraction of the dry powder medicament of at least 30%. 8. The medicament delivery device of any of claims 1 to 7, wherein maximum synthetic jetting is achieved within about 1,000 ms or less from the start of a transducer activation so that the dry powder medicament becomes aerosolized and delivered from the dosing chamber via synthetic jetting over a transducer on-time of about 5 seconds or less. 9. The medicament delivery device of any of claims 1 to 8, wherein the transducer on-time includes between 2-15 transducer activations. 10. The medicament delivery device of any of claims 1 to 9, wherein the device delivers between about 0.1 mg to about 10 mg of dry powder medicament over an inhalation cycle including between 2-15 transducer activations. 11. The medicament delivery device of any of claims 1 to 10, wherein the dosing chamber comprises a tunnel configured to receive the medicament from a blister. 12. The medicament delivery device of any of claims 1 to 11 comprising a removable cartridge and a base, wherein the dry powder medicament is contained in the removable cartridge. 13. The medicament delivery device of claim 12, wherein the dry powder medicament is contained in a blister strip. 14. A method of using the medicament delivery device of any of claims 1 to 13 to administer a therapeutically effective amount of one or more dry powder medicaments comprising completing an inhalation cycle of consecutive inhalations from the device. 15. A method of treating a respiratory disease or disorder, or a symptom thereof, comprising completing an inhalation cycle of consecutive inhalations from the medicament delivery device of any of claims 1 to 13, wherein the device administers a therapeutically effective amount of one or more medicaments over the course of the inhalation cycle. 16. A method of increasing FEVi in a patient comprising completing an inhalation cycle of consecutive inhalations from the medicament delivery device of any of claims 1 to 13, wherein the device administers a therapeutically effective amount of one or more medicaments over the course of the inhalation cycle. 17. A method of treating COPD or a symptom thereof comprising completing an inhalation cycle of consecutive inhalations from the medicament delivery device of any of claims 1 to 13, wherein the device administers a therapeutically effective amount of one or more medicaments over the course of the inhalation cycle, wherein the one or more medicaments are selected from the group comprising a LAMA, a LAB A, a SABA, a corticosteroid, and a combination thereof. 18. A method of treating asthma or a symptom thereof comprising completing an inhalation cycle of consecutive inhalations from the medicament delivery device of any of claims 1 to 13, wherein the device administers a therapeutically effective amount of one or more medicaments over the course of the inhalation cycle, wherein the one or more medicaments are selected from the group comprising a LAMA, a LAB A, a SABA, a corticosteroid, and a combination thereof. 19. A method of treating cystic fibrosis or a symptom thereof comprising completing an inhalation cycle of consecutive inhalations from the medicament delivery device of any of claims 1 to 13, wherein the device administers a therapeutically effective amount of one or more antibiotics over the course of the inhalation cycle. 20. A method of treating cystic fibrosis or a symptom thereof comprising completing an inhalation cycle of consecutive inhalations from the medicament delivery device of any of claims 1 

 to 13, wherein the device administers a therapeutically effective amount of DNase over the course of the inhalation cycle. 21. A method of treating idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis or a symptom thereof comprising completing an inhalation cycle of consecutive inhalations from the medicament delivery device of any of claims 1 to 13, wherein the device administers a therapeutically effective amount of pirfenidone over the course of the inhalation cycle. 22. A medicament delivery device comprising: a dosing chamber comprising an interior that is configured to contain dry powder medicament, and a transducer confronting the dosing chamber, wherein the dosing chamber and transducer are acoustically resonant such that the dosing chamber resonates in response to an activation of the transducer at a frequency of about 37-42 kHz; wherein the dosing chamber has an interior shape, internal height and location of one or more openings configured to cause the dry powder medicament to become aerosolized and delivered from the dosing chamber via synthetic jetting upon an activation of the transducer, wherein the dosing chamber's interior shape is at least partially defined by a lower sidewall that transitions to a shoulder, the shoulder transitions to an apex extending away from the lower sidewall, and the apex converges to a point, wherein the one or more openings in the dosing chamber are disposed in the apex, and wherein the internal height of the dosing chamber is configured so that the one or more openings are disposed at one or more anti-nodes of the dosing chamber when the transducer is activated, so that pressure oscillation at the one or more anti-nodes is sufficiently high to cause the dry powder medicament to become aerosolized and delivered from the one or more openings, wherein maximum synthetic jetting is achievable within about 500 ms or less from the start of a transducer activation so that the dry powder medicament is deliverable from the dosing chamber via synthetic jetting over 2 to 30 transducer activations and a transducer on-time of about 5 seconds or less.
